questions.q: On which date did the Goods and Services Tax come into effect in India?
questions.explanation: The Goods and Services Tax was rolled out across India on 1 July 2017.

questions.q: GST is a tax levied on which of the following?
options: The supply of goods and services
questions.explanation: GST is a comprehensive indirect tax levied on the supply of goods and services.

questions.q: GST is best described as which type of tax?
questions.explanation: GST is an indirect tax that replaced many earlier indirect taxes.

questions.q: Which slogan is commonly associated with the introduction of GST in India?
questions.explanation: GST was promoted under the slogan 'One Nation, One Tax', reflecting its unifying aim.

questions.q: The body that recommends rates, exemptions and rules for GST is the:
questions.explanation: The GST Council makes recommendations on rates, exemptions and procedures relating to GST.

questions.q: GST replaced many earlier taxes into a single system; this process is best described as:
questions.explanation: GST unified a number of central and state indirect taxes into one system.

questions.q: GST is administered with the help of a council that brings together the Union and the:
questions.explanation: The GST Council is a joint forum of the Union and the states for matters relating to GST.

questions.q: The portion of GST collected by the central government on an intra-state supply is called:
questions.explanation: On an intra-state supply, the central component is the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST).

questions.q: The portion of GST collected by a state government on an intra-state supply is called:
questions.explanation: On an intra-state supply, the state component is the State Goods and Services Tax (SGST).

questions.q: The tax levied on the inter-state supply of goods and services under the GST system is the:
questions.explanation: Inter-state supplies are taxed under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST).

questions.q: GST was introduced through which Constitutional Amendment Act?
questions.explanation: GST was enabled by the Constitution (One Hundred and First Amendment) Act, 2016.

questions.q: Which Article of the Constitution, inserted by the GST amendment, provides for the GST Council?
questions.explanation: Article 279A provides for the constitution of the GST Council.

questions.q: Who is the ex-officio Chairperson of the GST Council?
questions.explanation: The Union Finance Minister is the Chairperson of the GST Council.

questions.q: Which new Article inserted by the GST amendment grants concurrent power to the Union and states to levy GST?
questions.explanation: Article 246A confers concurrent power on the Union and the states to make laws on GST.

questions.q: GST in India is structured as which type of tax model?
options: A dual GST shared by Centre and states
questions.explanation: India adopted a dual GST model in which both the Centre and the states levy tax on the same supply.

questions.q: The mechanism under GST that allows businesses to set off tax paid on inputs against tax on output is called:
questions.explanation: Input tax credit lets registered businesses offset tax paid on inputs against their output tax liability.

questions.q: Which earlier central levy was subsumed into GST?
questions.explanation: Central Excise Duty was among the central indirect taxes subsumed into GST.

questions.q: Which earlier state-level tax was subsumed into GST?
questions.explanation: State Value Added Tax (VAT) was among the state taxes subsumed into GST.

questions.q: In the GST Council, decisions are taken by which kind of majority of weighted votes?
options: Three-fourths of weighted votes present and voting
A two-thirds majority of all states
questions.explanation: Decisions of the GST Council require at least three-fourths of the weighted votes of members present and voting.

questions.q: In the GST Council's voting structure, the votes of all the state governments together carry what share of the total weight?
questions.explanation: The states' votes together carry two-thirds of the weight, while the Centre's vote carries one-third.

questions.q: Which goods were kept outside the purview of GST at its introduction, remaining under earlier taxes?
options: Petroleum products and alcohol for human consumption
questions.explanation: Petroleum products and alcoholic liquor for human consumption were kept outside GST at its launch.

questions.q: The online platform that handles GST registration, returns and payments is the:
questions.explanation: The Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN) provides the IT backbone for GST processes.

questions.q: The GST amendment inserted a definition of 'goods and services tax' into the Constitution through clause (12A) of which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 366(12A), inserted by the 101st Amendment, defines 'goods and services tax' as a tax on the supply of goods or services or both, except taxes on the supply of alcoholic liquor for human consumption.

questions.q: Which Article inserted by the GST amendment provides for compensation to states for revenue loss in the initial years?
options: Section 18 of the amendment providing compensation
questions.explanation: Section 18 of the 101st Amendment Act provided for compensating states for revenue loss for a transition period, given effect by a separate compensation law.

questions.q: Under GST, the tax levied on supplies within a union territory without a legislature is the:
questions.explanation: Union Territory GST (UTGST) applies to supplies within union territories without their own legislature.

questions.q: The Article inserted by the GST amendment that deals with the levy and apportionment of GST on inter-state trade is:
questions.explanation: Article 269A deals with the levy and apportionment of GST on inter-state supplies between the Union and the states.

questions.q: GST was launched at a special midnight session held in which venue on 30 June-1 July 2017?
questions.explanation: GST was formally launched at a midnight session in the Central Hall of Parliament.

questions.q: GST first applied to the state of Jammu and Kashmir slightly after the rest of the country because it required:
options: A separate enactment by its own legislature
questions.explanation: Owing to its special constitutional position at the time, GST was extended to Jammu and Kashmir through a separate enactment shortly after 1 July 2017.

questions.q: The committee of officials that supports the GST Council by examining issues before they are placed before it is known as the:
questions.explanation: Officer committees such as the Fitment Committee and Law Committee examine rate and legal issues before the GST Council.

questions.q: The Constitution (One Hundred and First Amendment) Act enabling GST received the President's assent in which year?
questions.explanation: The 101st Amendment Act received presidential assent in 2016, with GST rolled out on 1 July 2017.
